boo: interjection. A loud exclamation intended to scare someone.
book: noun. A collection of sheets of paper bound together to hinge at one edge, containing printed or written material, pictures, etc.
bookshop: noun. A shop that sells books.
bop: noun. A very light smack, blow or punch.
hob: noun. A kind of cutting tool, used to cut the teeth of a gear.
hobo: noun. A wandering homeless person, especially one illegally travelling by rail or a penniless, unemployed bum.
hook: noun. A rod bent into a curved shape, typically with one end free and the other end secured to a rope or other attachment.
hoop: noun. A circular band of metal used to bind a barrel.
kob: noun. An African antelope closely related to the lechwe and the waterbuck, Kobus kob.
kobo: noun. A subdivision of currency, equal to one hundredth of a Nigerian naira.
oho: interjection. Expressing surprise or gloating realisation; aha.
oohs: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of ooh
oops: interjection. Used to acknowledge a small mistake or accident.
poh: interjection. Indicating that something is trivial and not worth consideration.
pooh: interjection. Expressing dismissal, contempt, impatience, etc.
poohs: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of pooh
posh: adjective. Associated with the upper classes.
shoo: verb. To induce someone or something to leave.
shook: noun. A set of pieces for making a cask or box, usually wood.
shop: noun. An establishment that sells goods or services to the public; originally only a physical location, but now a virtual establishment as
sob: noun. A cry with a short, sudden expulsion of breath.
boh: interjection. An exclamation used to startle or frighten.
boohs: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of booh
kbps: noun. Initialism of kilobits per second.
oks: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Oko-Eni-Osayen.
oop: interjection. Used to acknowledge a small mistake or accident.
pbs: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Central Pame.
pho: noun. A Vietnamese soup with a beef base, typically served with rice noodles and optionally beef or chicken.
phoo: interjection. An expression of rejection or disgust.
pobs: noun. Pieces of bread soaked in hot milk used as a comfort food for children during illness
poo: noun. Alternative spelling of pooh: an instance of saying " poo ".
shoop: noun. The fruit of the rose; rosehip.
shopbook: noun. A book in which a tradesman keeps the accounts.
shp: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Shipibo-Conibo.
soho: interjection. ho; halloo; a word used in calling from a distant place.
soko: noun. A species of African ape, supposedly a variety of the chimpanzee.
